item.title: Tailscale + Paperless-ngx: scan everything, expose nothing
item.description: Set up Paperless-ngx with Tailscale to store and securely access your tax, medical, and other documents, with optional AI tagging.

item.title: This month at Tailscale for April 2026
item.description: What's new in Tailscale clients this month, including Aperture features, Kubernetes improvements, and API-only tailnet access.

item.title: Aperture beta: better controls for the AI agent era
item.description: Now available on all plans, Aperture offers better controls and visibility—just in time for the end of subsidized AI.

item.title: Meet tailscale-rs, our new Rust library preview
item.description: An early preview of tailscale-rs is out now, and we’d like your help testing what comes next.

item.title: Pricing v4: more value, more simply
item.description: Tailscale’s updated plans are now live. The free Personal plan is more generous, business plans include more features, and pricing is easier to understand and predict.

item.title: Being the adult in the room
item.description: The industry is moving quickly. The hard part is not losing your footing.

item.title: The hidden costs of “good enough” network access
item.description: When infrastructure is simplified, the benefits scale across the entire organization: IT efficiency, worker productivity, reduced breach exposure, and serious ROI.

item.title: This month at Tailscale for March 2026
item.description: What's new in Tailscale clients this month, including Tailscale Peer Relay and Services integrations, windowed UI on macOS, and more.

item.title: Escaping the notch: Tailscale's new macOS home
item.description: How Tailscale tells macOS users that our menu bar icon has been swallowed by The Notch—and why our new UI makes it less of an issue.

item.title: OpenClaw is fun. OpenClaw is dangerous. Here's where Tailscale helps.
item.description: Lots of people are using Tailscale to reach their OpenClaw setups. We can’t make OpenClaw “safe,” but we can remove a few sharp edges.

item.title: Aperture by Tailscale: More secure AI now available via self-serve
item.description: Spin up Aperture on your own to centralize, control, and learn how AI is used across your organization.

item.title: Border0 is joining Tailscale
item.description: Border0 has been building a "PAM starter pack" on top of Tailscale that provides tight access controls, good visibility, and an audit trail you can trust. Now they're officially part of Tailscale.

item.title: Behind the Winter Update: The team that made it work
item.description: A look behind the scenes at the people who turned product releases into real impact.

item.title: LM Link: Use local models on remote devices, powered by Tailscale
item.description: LM Link is the easy and more secure way to share LLMs between devices you control, unexposed to the public Internet.

item.title: Making infrastructure access lighter, simpler, and smarter
item.description: Tailscale's latest updates treat secure connectivity, access control, and audit observability as one cohesive system, reducing complexity while keeping strict standards.
